policy_statement: The National Adaptation Plan (NAP) Process will serve as a 10-year blueprint (2020–2030) guiding adaptation planning and implementation, with a review around 2029 and a new framework developed for 2031–2040.
verbatim_text: The NAP Process (this  document)  will  serve  as  a  blueprint  to  guide  the  overall  process  and  provide structure.  This NAP Process will be in effect for ten years (2020-2030), during which time there will be ongoing monitoring and evaluation,  in approximately 2029, this first period  of  implementation  will be reviewed, and a new framework will be developed to guide the period from 2031-2040.

policy_statement: Liberia’s NAP Process (2020–2030) will reduce vulnerability and build adaptive capacity/resilience, and facilitate integration of climate change adaptation into fiscal, regulatory, and development policies, programs, and activities.
verbatim_text:
The  road  map  provides  a  guideline  for  implementing the NAP process in Liberia and working in the short, medium, and long term. The objectives of the NAP process are:

- i. to  reduce  vulnerability  to  the  adverse  impacts  of  climate  change  by  building  adaptive capacity and resilience; and,
- ii. to  facilitate  the  integration  of  climate  change  adaptation  into  fiscal,  regulatory,  and development policies, programs, and activities (UNFCCC, 2012).
extraction_rationale: States explicit objectives of the national NAP process, including integration into fiscal/regulatory/development policy domains (a concrete governance commitment).
reasoning: The statement describes high-level objectives of Liberia’s NAP process (reduce vulnerability; integrate adaptation into policies/programs). The verbatim text does not specify any measurable outcome (e.g., % reduction, number of plans updated) or any explicit deadline/year for achieving these objectives; it also reads as guidance/objectives rather than a binding mandate. Therefore it is not auditable/measurable under the strict individual-policy rule and should be classified as NON-SOUND.
validation_reasoning: Quantifiable target: No. The text lists objectives—“to reduce vulnerability… by building adaptive capacity and resilience” and “to facilitate the integration of climate change adaptation into fiscal, regulatory, and development policies, programs, and activities”—but provides no measurable metric (no %/counts/coverage). Timeline: No. Although it mentions “short, medium, and long term,” there is no explicit deadline or year in the verbatim excerpt. Binding mechanism: No. The excerpt describes a “road map” that “provides a guideline for implementing the NAP process” and states “objectives,” without legal/mandatory terms indicating enforceable requirements. Spatial specificity: Yes, it applies to “in Liberia” / “the NAP process in Liberia.” Weak language: Yes, phrases like “provides a guideline” and “to facilitate” indicate non-binding intent without hard targets. Strong language: No, there are no mandate/require terms paired with numbers. Because it lacks both a quantifiable target and a timeline, it fails the gold-standard requirement and is NON-SOUND.

policy_statement: The GCF-funded NAP project will focus on four outputs: strengthening institutional frameworks/coordination for NAP implementation, expanding the knowledge base for scaling up adaptation, building capacity to mainstream adaptation into planning and budgeting, and formulating financing mechanisms for scaling up adaptation.
verbatim_text:
The  activities  in  this  project  focus  on  four outputs:

- i. Strengthening institutional frameworks and coordination for implementation of the NAPs.
- ii. Expansion of the knowledge base for scaling up adaptation.
- iii. Building  capacity  for  mainstreaming  climate  change  adaptation  into  planning  and budgeting processes; and
- iv. systems formulation of financing mechanisms for scaling up adaptation (including public, private, national, and international)
